verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted ligand that exists in latent and active forms. The latent complex (mature dimer + LAP + LTBP) remains cell-surface-associated and extracellularly anchored via covalent disulfide and non-covalent interactions with LTBP (a matrix-associated TM/ECM protein), creating a wash-resistant surface pool. The mature active dimer is released by proteolytic activation and signaling. The latent surface-anchored pool is the primary biological form in many tissues and is targeted by therapeutic programs; thus the protein qualifies as contextually surface-accessible via latent-complex tethering.
